title: Japan's tax revenue reaches a record 84 trillion yen
summary: The figure exceeds the previous year for the ninth consecutive year, but the extra revenue is not enough to compensate for the tax cuts.

title: Japanese parliament remains paralyzed without agreement to resume sessions
summary: The ruling party and opposition accuse each other of blocking the legislative agenda, including the reform of the Imperial House Law.

title: Japan's long-term interest rate reaches 2.81%, its highest level in 30 years
summary: The increase is due to inflation expectations and the government's expansionary fiscal policy.

title: Japan's wage increase exceeds 5% for third consecutive year
summary: The spring negotiations culminated in an average increase of 5.01%, although slightly lower than the previous year.

title: Reiwa party leader Taro Yamamoto indicted for serious speeding
summary: He was driving at 149 km/h on an Oita highway, resulting in a fine of 90,000 yen and suspension of his driver's license.

title: Father arrested for death of his wife and daughter in Nagano
summary: Police reported that both died from asphyxiation and the man was arrested after his hospitalization.

title: Influencer Bobby Ologon indicted for non-consensual sex
summary: The prosecution accuses him of taking advantage of a massage to commit the crime.

title: Intermediaries in the murder of a couple in Tokyo sentenced to 30 years in prison
summary: The Tokyo court issued sentences for those who coordinated the death of restaurant owners in Ueno.

title: Former boxing world champion Takayoshi Yakushiji indicted for falsifying an adoption
summary: He submitted an adoption request for a woman he was involved with without his wife's permission.

title: Man rescued alive eight days after earthquake in Venezuela
summary: The earthquake has caused more than 2,500 deaths and the discovery of the survivor is considered a miracle.

title: Japan Airlines plane makes emergency landing in Kansai due to oil leak
summary: The aircraft flying from Haneda to Itami diverted its route and landed without injuries, temporarily closing the runway.

title: Tokyo celebrates the 250th anniversary of US independence with fireworks
summary: The event was attended by Governor Koike and Minister Koizumi, and a message from former President Trump.

title: Emperor and Empress report to Emperor Emeritus and Empress Emerita on official tour of Europe
summary: Naruhito and Masako met with their predecessors to report on the outcome of their visit to the Netherlands and Belgium.

title: Japan plans to drastically increase residency fees for foreigners
summary: Most asylum seekers would be excluded from reductions, drawing criticism from support organizations.

title: Bidding begins for post-Expo 2025 development in Osaka
summary: The metropolitan government is seeking developers for the site, valued at around 100 billion yen.

title: Rail capacity to Narita Airport will double in the 2030s
summary: The plan responds to the expected increase in passengers due to the airport expansion.

title: Tanabe Pharma sells its manufacturing subsidiary to Towa Pharmaceutical
summary: The operation will allow Tanabe to focus on research and development of new drugs.

title: Ruling party proposes expanding communications surveillance in Japan
summary: The draft intelligence plan includes administrative interception to strengthen national security.

title: Yuto Nagatomo left out of FC Tokyo's new squad
summary: The J1 League club has announced its squad for the new season without including the experienced international full-back.

title: Advances in Chinese robotics: humanoid robot company goes public and realistic android presented
summary: Unitree, a robot manufacturer, obtained approval to list on the stock market, while Chinese researchers show a very realistic human-like android.
